title: NBA Daily Digest: March 31, 2026
subtitle: Your daily dose of hoops action and news!

items.headline: Trail Blazers Sold to Tom Dundon-Led Group
items.summary: The NBA Board of Governors has officially approved the sale of the Portland Trail Blazers from the estate of Paul Allen to a group led by Carolina Hurricanes owner Tom Dundon. The deal, valued at approximately $4.25 billion, is set to close by March 31.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade Deadline Looms for Bucks
items.summary: Milwaukee Bucks co-owner Wes Edens has reportedly given superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo until October 1, 2026, to sign a contract extension or face a trade this summer. The Knicks, Cavaliers, Rockets, and Magic are among the teams closely monitoring the situation.

items.headline: Chicago Bulls Project $60 Million in Offseason Cap Space
items.summary: The Chicago Bulls are poised for a significant offseason, with reports indicating they could open up approximately $60 million in cap space, giving them substantial flexibility for free agency or trades.

items.headline: Heat Top Sixers in High-Scoring Affair
Hawks Soar Past Celtics
Spurs Dominate Bulls
Suns Shine Bright Against Grizzlies
Wolves Cruise Past Mavericks
Cavs Hold Off Jazz Challenge
Thunder Edge Pistons in Overtime Thriller
LeBron's Triple-Double Leads Lakers Over Wizards
LeBron James Ties All-Time Wins Record with Triple-Double
items.summary: King James delivered a dominant performance with 21 points, 12 assists, and 10 rebounds, leading the Lakers to victory and tying Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's record for most career wins (1,228).

items.headline: Wemby Makes History with Record-Breaking Double-Double
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama showcased his generational talent with 41 points and 16 rebounds against the Bulls, securing his double-double in the fastest time in NBA history.

items.headline: SGA Drops 47 Points in Thunder OT Win
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander was unstoppable, pouring in 47 points to lead the Oklahoma City Thunder to an overtime victory against the Detroit Pistons.

items.headline: Booker's Efficient 36 Points Powers Suns
items.summary: Devin Booker was a scoring machine for the Suns, dropping 36 points on an efficient 16-of-24 shooting from the field.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Continues Strong All-Star Season
items.summary: Portland's Deni Avdija, a first-time All-Star this season, is averaging 23.8 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 6.7 assists per game. He previously posted 29 points, 9 rebounds, and 9 assists in a game against the Clippers on December 26.

items.headline: NBA Enters Stretch Run: Playoff Picture Clarifies
items.summary: With only 111 games remaining in the season, the playoff picture is taking shape. Detroit, Boston, Oklahoma City, and San Antonio have secured playoff berths, while New York, Cleveland, the Lakers, and Denver are on the verge of clinching. Portland and Golden State are locked into the play-in tournament.

items.headline: Luka Doncic Serves One-Game Suspension
items.summary: Dallas Mavericks star Luka Doncic is serving a one-game suspension after accumulating his 16th technical foul of the season.

items.headline: Celtics Eyeing Duke's Patrick Ngongba II in 2026 Draft
items.summary: The Boston Celtics are reportedly looking towards the 2026 NBA Draft, with Duke sophomore Patrick Ngongba II emerging as a potential target to bolster their frontcourt.

items.headline: Tonight's NBA Schedule: March 31, 2026
items.summary: Catch all the action tonight with a full slate of games, including Phoenix vs. Orlando, Charlotte vs. Brooklyn, Dallas vs. Milwaukee, New York vs. Houston, Toronto vs. Detroit, Cleveland vs. LA Lakers, and Portland vs. LA Clippers.
